items.title: Explosions as one of Australia's two oil refineries erupts in flames

children.content: Authorities were called to one of Australia's two operational oil refineries at about 11pm after residents reported "explosions and flames".

image.altText: Firefighters are battling a huge fire at an oil refinery in Geelong.
Fire has broken out at Geelong oil refinery.

gallery.altText: A plume of smoke rises into the air over the refinery, which is bathed in an orange glow

thumbnail.altText: A plume of smoke rises into the air over the refinery, which is bathed in an orange glow

items.title: Bowen says oil refinery fire will impact petrol production 'for some time'

image.altText: A plume of smoke rises into the air over the refinery, which is bathed in an orange glow

children.content: Energy Minister Chris Bowen tells the ABC that the fire at the Geelong oil refinery will impact petrol production "for some time". Follow live.

items.title: Iranian-flagged ship tries to evade US blockade as Pakistan mediators arrive in Tehran

children.content: The US president has said a deal with Iran is "preferable" and to expect an "amazing two days" ahead. Follow live.

image.altText: A US military ship

items.title: Talk of ceasefire in Lebanon as strait blockade continues: What to know today on Iran

children.content: Israeli forces are expected to "overwhelm" parts of southern Lebanon and yet a ceasefire may still be only a matter of days away.

image.altText: A man wearing a helmet and flak jacket with the word PRESS on it looks at the camera with building rubble behind him

items.title: Albanese government's frustration rises as Australians pay price for war

children.content: Fuel prices and inflation aren't the only metrics climbing as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut. The Albanese government's level of frustration is rising just as fast.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese in a jacket and tie

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Creators of fuel dashboard quoted by News Corp have links to Liberals

children.content: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

image.altText: A graph showing a plane, a fuel pump and a plane with a countdown in red.

items.title: Ukraine looking to 'capitalise' on new momentum against Russia

children.content: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

gallery.altText: A man standing by the ruins of a bombed out car in a car park.

thumbnail.altText: A man standing by the ruins of a bombed out car in a car park.

items.title: Defence spending to climb by $53 billion as 'international norms' erode

children.content: Australia's defence spending will climb by $53 billion over the next decade amid what the federal government says are "intensifying" global risks.

image.altText: Richard Marles

items.title: Fuel crisis set to worsen with massive fire at Geelong refinery

children.content: Our fuel crisis to worsen with massive fire at one of Australia's two oil refineries. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Supermarket milk price rises tipped to help hundreds of dairy farmers

children.content: Supermarket milk price rises linked to the war in Iran are predicted in the coming months as hundreds of dairy farmers battling increasing costs receive more for their product.

image.altText: A line of cows walk into one side of the bales to get ready to be milked.

items.title: Proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyer scheme slammed

children.content: Multicultural groups are concerned Angus Taylor's proposal to prevent permanent residents from accessing the Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme could deter skilled migrants from coming to Australia.

image.altText: Mr taylor, in suit and tie, speaks to the media in a passage at Parliament House.

items.title: Kerr scores landmark goal as Matildas win FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

gallery.altText: Female football players on the podium, captain in centre lifting trophy

thumbnail.altText: Female football players on the podium, captain in centre lifting trophy

items.title: A lucky chat, then a lifeline for men with prostate cancer

children.content: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

image.altText: A farmer in his 60s in a blue shirt stands in a paddock looking into the camera.

items.title: Woman suffers brain injury after 'toxic dose' of wrong drug

children.content: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

image.altText: A patient laying on a hospital bed, knees up with blanket covering their legs, holding a cup next to urine sample container

items.title: Back Roads: How this tiny town welcomed a 70-piece orchestra

children.content: The Sydney Youth Orchestras travelled to this tiny town to help inspire the local kids, but when they left, they were the ones who felt changed.

image.altText: Zhayle with a wide grin.

gallery.altText: Zhayle stands in front of a playground, holding a wind instrument.
Four girls wearing matching jumpers run towards the camera smiling.

items.title: Quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and E.R.?

children.content: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor E.R.?

image.altText: Composite image of Noah Wyle as a young and older doctor

items.title: A new layer of AI tech is turbocharging the fashion industry

children.content: Dynamic pricing is already changing the final cost of clothing. Some prices are rising, but others are falling.

image.altText: A person holds a phone and scrolls through a clothes shopping site

items.title: 'Canary in coal mine' warning as marine pest found in new area

children.content: The discovery on Tasmania's west coast has scientists and fishers alike fearing the spread of a destructive species to another previously untouched part of the state's marine environment.

items.title: Gina Rinehart 'won' the legal battle for Hope Downs, commentator says

children.content: Gina Rinehart and her company, Hancock Prospecting, are the "clear winners" of what prominent Perth industry commentator Tim Treadgold said was the "big daddy" of legal battles.

image.altText: A graphic with several faces

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: composite image of two women looking at paperwork

items.title: Dozens of workers with disability made redundant by award-winning charity

children.content: Help Enterprises made 41 positions redundant weeks after it was named Brisbane's social enterprise of the year.

image.altText: A close up of a man's hands in his lap.

items.title: Manhunt underway after prisoner mistakenly released from jail

children.content: Kyle Quayle is believed to be in the Newcastle area and officers from the Newcastle City Police District are asking the public for assistance as they continue to try to find him.

image.altText: head shot of a man with medium length brown hair, orange clothes

items.title: Henderson 'content' knowing her epic reign will not last forever

children.content: Rebecca Henderson has won 13 elite national mountain bike cross-country titles in a row and, this season, is unbeaten in the UCI Oceania Continental series, which concludes this weekend at the Cairns Rainforest Rumble.

Rebecca Henderson jumps over a log
Rebecca Henderson goes over a mound on her bike

Rebecca Henderson goes over a mound on her bike

items.title: Adam was quoted $60,000 for his home insurance. His local council is taking action

children.content: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

image.altText: man in blue workshirt walking along the river

items.title: At least nine dead, including eight children, in Türkiye school shooting

children.content: A boy, believed to be aged about 13, carried out the shooting in southeastern Türkiye, killing his fellow students and a teacher. It is the second school shooting in the country in two days.

image.altText: turkiye school shooting

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

image.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: Short-stay levy overperforms, as holiday rental growth stagnates in Victoria

children.content: Data suggests the growth in short-stay accommodation listings in Victoria has slowed in the year after the Victorian government's new tax came into effect.

image.altText: AB town

items.title: How the technology we use every day is changing our brains

children.content: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

gallery.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

items.title: Farmer feels 'disrespected' over VicGrid's attempt to access land

children.content: VicGrid escalates its attempts to access land for VNI West surveys, threatening fines or court-ordered entry to farmers who stand in the way.

image.altText: VicGrid officers and farmer Nathan McKnight

items.title: Hospital review identifies series of errors before man's death by suicide

children.content: Blake Whittington, 23, was taken to hospital after a suicide attempt. He was discharged, then referred to a support service that did not contact him until days after he took his own life.

image.altText: a framed picture of Blake Whittington being hedl bu his mother at a cemetery

items.title: Lesbian Action Group wins appeal in case seeking to ban trans women

children.content: The Federal Court allows a lesbian action group's appeal to be allowed to legally exclude transgender women from its public events.

image.altText: Lesbian Action Group celebrate the win outside court

items.title: Which shade of white is right? Dentists explain the teeth colours

children.content: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

image.altText: A close up image of a woman looking at her teeth in a small mirror

items.title: 'Face lift' for Queensland dinosaur after discovery of 1,300 more bones

children.content: The dinosaur's face lift has become the butt of online jokes among palaeontology fans, but researchers say it is the most accurate depiction of the creature yet.
